commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the officers shall speak further unto the people, and they shall say, What man is there that is fearful and fainthearted. let him go and return unto his house, lest his brethren's heart faint as well as his heart. Such often occasion the loss of battles. Alexander sent away all who had not courage to follow him in his expeditions. ) The Rabbins condemn these faint-hearted soldiers to carry water, for the army, to prepare the roads and places for encampments. (Calmet) But this seems contrary to the intention of the lawgiver, who sends them back to their houses.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
